headline: Qualcomm China Trip Puts AI And Chip Export Risks In Focus
summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon is joining a U.S. business delegation on President Donald Trump's upcoming summit trip to China for talks with Xi Jinping. The delegation, which also includes leaders from Tesla and Apple, is focused on pursuing business agreements and discussing U.S. access to the Chinese market. The visit is expected to touch on semiconductor export rules and commercial ties between the U.S. and China. For investors watching NasdaqGS:QCOM, this trip sits at the intersection of...

headline: TikTok challenges EU 'gatekeeper' status at Europe's top court
summary: Bytedance's social media platform TikTok on Tuesday made a last- ditch attempt at Europe's top court to scrap its ‌designation as a "gatekeeper" under EU rules, which require it to meet tougher standards ‌as regulators seek to curb the power of Big Tech.  The case--the first EU Digital Markets Act "gatekeeper" challenge heard by ​the EU Court of Justice--could bolster or weaken Europe's attempt to rein in Big Tech to ensure competition and more choice for users.

content.title: TikTok challenges EU 'gatekeeper' status at Europe's top court
content.summary: Bytedance's social media platform TikTok on Tuesday made a last- ditch attempt at Europe's top court to scrap its ‌designation as a "gatekeeper" under EU rules, which require it to meet tougher standards ‌as regulators seek to curb the power of Big Tech.  The case--the first EU Digital Markets Act "gatekeeper" challenge heard by ​the EU Court of Justice--could bolster or weaken Europe's attempt to rein in Big Tech to ensure competition and more choice for users.

headline: Nvidia Stock Falls After Report CEO Huang Is Excluded From Trump’s China Trip
summary: Nvidia  stock looked set to fall on Tuesday from a record high in the previous session.  CEO Jensen Huang won’t be among the business leaders accompanying President Donald Trump on this week’s visit to China, according to reports.  The recent strength of the semiconductor sector has been a boon for Nvidia, as investors warm again to the prospects for its artificial-intelligence chips.

headline: Nvidia’s Huang Wants to Sell Chips to China. Trump Has Other Priorities.

It might be a sign that the White House is giving up on hopes of opening up the Chinese market for U.S. chip companies.  The recent strength of the semiconductor sector has been a boon for Nvidia, as investors warm again to the prospects for its artificial-intelligence chips.
